posts.hook: amazon's own billing system briefly said you owed $1.5 trillion
posts.caption: an aws pricing bug hands out trillion-dollar invoices, moonshot's own benchmarks now claim kimi k3 beats claude and gpt-5.5 at coding, apple opens doj settlement talks, and flock kills its ai-microphone screaming detector. full digest at sift.yasint.dev (link in bio)

slides.title: aws billing bug quotes some customers $1.5 trillion
slides.desc: one uk customer's usual sub-1-pound bill briefly read 5.8 billion pounds

slides.title: kimi k3's own benchmarks now claim wins over claude and gpt-5.5
slides.desc: a day after independent tests called the same model competitive, not best

slides.title: apple and the doj open talks to settle a 2024 antitrust suit
slides.desc: a separate legal track from apple's fight with ex-staff now at openai

slides.title: claude fable 5 comes to more anthropic plans on july 20
slides.desc: max and team premium get it at 50% of limits, pro users get usage credits

slides.title: flock ends its ai microphones that listened for screaming
slides.desc: the surveillance vendor reverses course after eff and community pushback

slides.title: cloudflare shields wordpress from a critical code-execution bug
slides.desc: coordinated fix ships before attackers get details of an unauthenticated exploit

slides.title: chips and nuclear power both draw fresh billions this week
slides.desc: etched raises near $20b for ai chips, valar atomics near $5b for reactors
